web_text: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways, has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds. His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, who, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high; having become so much better than the angels, as he has inherited a more excellent name than they have.
slang_text: God, who used to speak to our ancestors through the prophets, over and over, in all kinds of ways, has now, at the tail end of these days, spoken to us straight-up through His own Son — the one He appointed heir of everything, the one He made the whole tubular universe through. His Son's the radiance of His glory, the exact image of who He is, holdin' everything together by the power of His word, no lie. After He scrubbed us squeaky clean of our sins all by Himself, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty up on high — endin' up totally, way better than the angels, since He inherited a name way more epic than theirs, fer sure.

web_text:
For to which of the angels did he say at any time,
“You are my Son.
Today I have become your father?”
and again,
“I will be to him a Father,
and he will be to me a Son?” When he again brings in the firstborn into the world he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him.” Of the angels he says,
“Who makes his angels winds,
and his servants a flame of fire.” But of the Son he says,
“Your throne, O God, is forever and ever.
The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your Kingdom.
slang_text: No way, seriously — which angel did God ever say this to: "You're my Son. Today I've become your Father"? Or again: "I'll be a Father to Him, and He'll be a Son to me"? And when He brings the firstborn into the world again, He says, "Let every last angel of God worship Him." About the angels, He says, "He makes His angels into winds, and His servants into flames of fire, righteous and wild." But about the Son, He says, "Your throne, O God, lasts forever and ever, no doubt. The scepter of your Kingdom is a scepter of pure righteousness.

web_text:
You have loved righteousness, and hated iniquity;
therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.” And,
“You, Lord, in the beginning, laid the foundation of the earth.
The heavens are the works of your hands. They will perish, but you continue.
They all will grow old like a garment does. You will roll them up like a mantle,
and they will be changed;
but you are the same.
Your years will not fail.”
slang_text: You loved righteousness and hated sin, big time; that's why God, your God, anointed you with the oil of gladness, way above everybody else." And: "You, Lord, laid down the foundation of the earth way back at the start, before there even was a start. The heavens are the work of your own hands. They're gonna perish, but you're gonna stick around, fer sure. They're all gonna get grody and wear out like an old jacket. You'll roll 'em up like a mantle, and they'll get swapped out for somethin' new — but you stay exactly the same. Your years never run dry, no lie."

web_text:
But which of the angels has he told at any time,
“Sit at my right hand,
until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet?” Aren’t they all serving spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?
slang_text: But which angel did God ever tell, "Sit at my right hand, till I make your enemies your own personal footstool"? As if! Aren't they all just servin' spirits, dispatched to help the folks who are gonna inherit salvation?

web_text: Therefore we ought to pay greater attention to the things that were heard, lest perhaps we drift away. For if the word spoken through angels proved steadfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense; how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation—which at the first having been spoken through the Lord, was confirmed to us by those who heard; God also testifying with them, both by signs and wonders, by various works of power, and by gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to his own will?
slang_text: So we oughta pay way more attention to what we've already heard, or we might drift right off course, no lie. 'Cause if the message spoken through angels held up solid, and every screwup and act of disobedience got paid back fair and square — how are we gonna dodge it if we blow off a salvation this epic? It got spoken first through the Lord, and confirmed to us by the folks who heard it firsthand, with God backin' it up through signs, wonders, all kinds of tubular works of power, and gifts of the Holy Spirit, however He wanted to hand 'em out.

web_text:
For he didn’t subject the world to come, of which we speak, to angels. But one has somewhere testified, saying,
“What is man, that you think of him?
Or the son of man, that you care for him? You made him a little lower than the angels.
You crowned him with glory and honor. You have put all things in subjection under his feet.”
For in that he subjected all things to him, he left nothing that is not subject to him. But now we don’t see all things subjected to him, yet.
slang_text: 'Cause He didn't put the world that's comin', the one we're talkin' about, under the angels' control, no way. But somebody testified somewhere: "What's a person, that you'd even think about him? Or a son of man, that you'd care about him? You made him a little lower than the angels. You crowned him with glory and honor, righteous and choice. You put everything under his feet." In puttin' everything under him, God left nothin' that's not under his control. But right now, we don't see everything under his control yet, fer sure.

web_text:
But we see him who has been made a little lower than the angels, Jesus, because of the suffering of death crowned with glory and honor, that by the grace of God he should taste of death for everyone. For it became him, for whom are all things, and through whom are all things, in bringing many children to glory, to make the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings. For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all from one, for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brothers, saying,
“I will declare your name to my brothers.
Among of the congregation I will sing your praise.”
slang_text: But we see Him, Jesus, who got made a little lower than the angels, and because He suffered death, got crowned with glory and honor, epic and for real — so by God's grace He could taste death for everybody. It just made sense that God, who everything's for and everything's through, would make the source of their salvation complete through sufferin', while bringin' a ton of kids to glory. 'Cause the one who makes folks holy and the ones who get made holy all come from the same source — that's why He's not ashamed to call 'em brothers, no lie, sayin', "I'll announce your name to my brothers. I'll sing your praise right in the middle of the congregation."

web_text: Again, “I will put my trust in him.” Again, “Behold, here I am with the children whom God has given me.” Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in the same way partook of the same, that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil, and might deliver all of them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage. For most certainly, he doesn’t give help to angels, but he gives help to the offspring of Abraham.
slang_text: And again: "I'm gonna put my trust in Him." And again: "Check it out, here I am with the kids God gave me." Since the kids are all made of flesh and blood, He took on flesh and blood too, same as them, so through death He could totally wipe out the one who's got the power of death — that's the devil — and rescue everybody who spent their whole lives locked up in bondage 'cause they were scared stiff of dyin'. 'Cause fer sure, He doesn't help out angels — He helps out Abraham's own bloodline.

web_text: Therefore he was obligated in all things to be made like his brothers, that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make atonement for the sins of the people. For in that he himself has suffered being tempted, he is able to help those who are tempted.
slang_text: That's why He had to become like His brothers in every way, no lie, so He could turn into a merciful and faithful high priest in everything that matters to God, to pay for the people's sins. 'Cause since He Himself went through temptation and suffered for it, He's able to help everybody else who's gettin' tempted too, fer sure.

web_text: Therefore, holy brothers, partakers of a heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus; who was faithful to him who appointed him, as also was Moses in all his house. For he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses, because he who built the house has more honor than the house. For every house is built by someone; but he who built all things is God.
slang_text: So, holy brothers, sharin' in a heavenly callin' — think about Jesus, the Apostle and High Priest we confess, righteous through and through. He stayed faithful to the One who appointed Him, same as Moses stayed faithful in God's whole house. Jesus earned way more glory than Moses did, 'cause whoever builds the house gets more honor than the house itself, no duh. Every house gets built by somebody, sure — but the one who built everything, that's God.

web_text:
Moses indeed was faithful in all his house as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were afterward to be spoken, but Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are, if we hold fast our confidence and the glorying of our hope firm to the end. Therefore, even as the Holy Spirit says,
“Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion,
like as in the day of the trial in the wilderness,
slang_text: Moses stayed faithful in God's whole house, sure, but as a servant, testifyin' about stuff that'd get spoken later. But Christ stayed faithful as a Son runnin' the whole show — and we're the house, long as we hold onto our confidence and keep braggin' about our hope, solid, all the way to the end. So just like the Holy Spirit says: "Today, if you hear His voice, don't harden your hearts like you did back in the rebellion, way harsh as it was, like that day of testin' out in the wilderness,

web_text:
where your fathers tested me by proving me,
and saw my deeds for forty years. Therefore I was displeased with that generation,
and said, ‘They always err in their heart,
but they didn’t know my ways;’ as I swore in my wrath,
‘They will not enter into my rest.’” Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief, in falling away from the living God;
slang_text: where your ancestors tested me, tryin' to prove me, and watched what I did for forty straight years. That's why I got fed up with that bogus generation, and said, 'They keep messin' up in their hearts, and they never even figured out my ways'; so I swore, madder than mad, 'They're not gettin' into my rest.'" Watch out, brothers, that none of you ends up with an evil, unbelievin' heart, wanderin' away from the livin' God — that'd be way harsh;

web_text:
but exhort one another day by day, so long as it is called “today”; lest any one of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin. For we have become partakers of Christ, if we hold fast the beginning of our confidence firm to the end: while it is said,
“Today if you will hear his voice,
don’t harden your hearts, as in the rebellion.” For who, when they heard, rebelled? No, didn’t all those who came out of Egypt by Moses?
slang_text: but instead, encourage each other day after day, long as it's still called "today," so none of you get hardened by sin's slick, grody con job. 'Cause we're all in this with Christ, long as we hold onto our confidence, solid, right up to the end — same as it says: "Today, if you hear His voice, don't harden your hearts like you did back in the rebellion." 'Cause who was it that heard and still rebelled? Wasn't it everybody who bounced outta Egypt with Moses?

web_text: With whom was he displeased forty years? Wasn’t it with those who sinned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness? To whom did he swear that they wouldn’t enter into his rest, but to those who were disobedient? We see that they were not able to enter in because of unbelief.
slang_text: Who was God fed up with for forty years? Wasn't it the ones who sinned, whose bodies dropped dead out in the wilderness, way harsh? Who'd He swear wouldn't get into His rest, except the disobedient ones? So we can see, plain as day, they couldn't get in 'cause of their unbelief.

web_text: Let us fear therefore, lest perhaps anyone of you should seem to have come short of a promise of entering into his rest. For indeed we have had good news preached to us, even as they also did, but the word they heard didn’t profit them, because it wasn’t mixed with faith by those who heard. For we who have believed do enter into that rest, even as he has said, “As I swore in my wrath, they will not enter into my rest”; although the works were finished from the foundation of the world. For he has said this somewhere about the seventh day, “God rested on the seventh day from all his works”;
slang_text: So let's stay on guard, so none of us end up comin' up short of the promise of enterin' His rest. 'Cause we got the Good News preached to us too, same as they did — but the word they heard didn't do 'em no good, 'cause it never got mixed with faith by the folks who heard it, no lie. But us, who've already believed, we DO get to enter that rest — same as He already said, "Like I swore in my anger, they're not gettin' into my rest" — even though the work was already finished way back at the foundation of the world. 'Cause He said this somewhere about the seventh day: "God kicked back and rested on the seventh day from all His work";

web_text:
and in this place again, “They will not enter into my rest.” Seeing therefore it remains that some should enter into it, and they to whom the good news was preached before failed to enter in because of disobedience, he again defines a certain day, today, saying through David so long a time afterward (just as has been said),
“Today if you will hear his voice,
don’t harden your hearts.” For if Joshua had given them rest, he would not have spoken afterward of another day.
slang_text: and in this same spot again, "They're not gettin' into my rest." So since it's still on the table for some folks to enter it, and the ones who first got the Good News preached to 'em blew it and failed to enter 'cause of disobedience, He sets another day again, callin' it "today," speakin' through David way later on (like it already got said): "Today, if you hear His voice, don't harden your hearts." 'Cause if Joshua had already given 'em rest, no way God woulda talked about another day after that.

web_text: There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God. For he who has entered into his rest has himself also rested from his works, as God did from his. Let us therefore give diligence to enter into that rest, lest anyone fall after the same example of disobedience. For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit, of both joints and marrow, and is able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
slang_text: So there's still a righteous Sabbath rest waitin' for God's people. 'Cause whoever's entered His rest has also rested from their own work, same as God rested from His. So let's hustle to enter that rest, so nobody falls into the same trap of disobedience. 'Cause God's word is alive and active, sharper than any double-edged sword, cuttin' clean through soul and spirit, joints and marrow, no joke, able to figure out exactly what's goin' on in your thoughts and your heart.

web_text: There is no creature that is hidden from his sight, but all things are naked and laid open before the eyes of him to whom we must give an account. Having then a great high priest, who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold tightly to our confession. For we don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin. Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and may find grace for help in time of need.
slang_text: Nothin's hidden from His sight — everything's laid bare, wide open, in front of the eyes of the One we gotta answer to. So since we've got a bodacious high priest who already passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let's hold onto our confession, tight, no lettin' go. 'Cause we don't got a high priest who can't relate to our weak spots — we got one who got tempted every way we do, and still came out totally sinless. So let's march right up, bold, to the throne of grace, so we can score mercy and find grace exactly when we need help, fer sure.

web_text: For every high priest, being taken from among men, is appointed for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins. The high priest can deal gently with those who are ignorant and going astray, because he himself is also surrounded with weakness. Because of this, he must offer sacrifices for sins for the people, as well as for himself. Nobody takes this honor on himself, but he is called by God, just like Aaron was.
slang_text: 'Cause every high priest gets picked from among regular people, appointed to represent people in stuff involvin' God, so he can offer gifts and sacrifices for sins. The high priest can go easy on the folks who're clueless and wanderin' off track, 'cause he's surrounded by his own weaknesses too, no lie. That's why he's gotta offer sacrifices for the people's sins, and his own sins too. Nobody just grabs this honor for themselves like some wannabe — you gotta get called by God, same as Aaron was.

web_text:
So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest, but it was he who said to him,
“You are my Son.
Today I have become your father.” As he says also in another place,
“You are a priest forever,
after the order of Melchizedek.” He, in the days of his flesh, having offered up prayers and petitions with strong crying and tears to him who was able to save him from death, and having been heard for his godly fear, though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.
slang_text: Same deal with Christ — He didn't glorify Himself to become high priest, no way. It was God who told Him, "You're my Son. Today I've become your Father." And in another spot He says, "You're a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek." In the days He walked around in the flesh, He offered up prayers and requests with loud cryin' and tears to the One who could save Him from death, and He got heard, fer sure, 'cause of His godly reverence. Even though He was a Son, He still learned obedience through all the sufferin' He went through.

web_text: Having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation, named by God a high priest after the order of Melchizedek. About him we have many words to say, and hard to interpret, seeing you have become dull of hearing. For although by this time you should be teachers, you again need to have someone teach you the rudiments of the first principles of the revelations of God. You have come to need milk, and not solid food.
slang_text: Once He got made complete, He turned into the source of eternal salvation for everybody who obeys Him, named by God as high priest in the order of Melchizedek. There's a ton more we could say about Him, hard stuff to unpack, since you've gotten kinda slow — dare we say wastoid — at listenin'. 'Cause by now you oughta be teachers, but instead you need somebody to re-teach you the basics all over again, the first principles of what God's revealed. Way bogus, but you've ended up needin' milk instead of solid food.

web_text: For everyone who lives on milk is not experienced in the word of righteousness, for he is a baby. But solid food is for those who are full grown, who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern good and evil.
slang_text: 'Cause everybody who's still on milk hasn't gotten experienced in the word of righteousness yet — they're basically a baby, no lie. But solid food's for the full-grown folks, who've trained their senses through practice to tell the difference between good and evil, fer sure.

web_text: Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let us press on to perfection—not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works, of faith toward God, of the teaching of baptisms, of laying on of hands, of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment. This will we do, if God permits. For concerning those who were once enlightened and tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Spirit,
slang_text: So let's leave the basic teachin's about Christ behind and push on to maturity, full speed ahead — not layin' down the foundation all over again: repentance from dead works, faith toward God, teachin' about baptisms, layin' on of hands, resurrection of the dead, eternal judgment. We'll do this, if God gives the green light. 'Cause for the folks who once got enlightened, tasted the heavenly gift, and shared in the Holy Spirit,

web_text: and tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the age to come, and then fell away, it is impossible to renew them again to repentance; seeing they crucify the Son of God for themselves again, and put him to open shame. For the land which has drunk the rain that comes often on it, and produces a crop suitable for them for whose sake it is also tilled, receives blessing from God; but if it bears thorns and thistles, it is rejected and near being cursed, whose end is to be burned.
slang_text: and tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age still comin', and then fell away — no way, it's impossible to bring 'em back to repentance again. They're basically crucifyin' the Son of God all over again for themselves, and shamin' Him in public, way harsh. 'Cause land that's soaked up all the rain that keeps fallin' on it, and grows a crop useful to whoever's farmin' it, gets a blessin' from God. But if it just grows thorns and thistles, it's bogus, worthless, close to gettin' cursed, and it's headed for the fire.

web_text: But, beloved, we are persuaded of better things for you, and things that accompany salvation, even though we speak like this. For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work and the labor of love which you showed toward his name, in that you served the saints, and still do serve them. We desire that each one of you may show the same diligence to the fullness of hope even to the end, that you won’t be sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and perseverance inherited the promises.
slang_text: But, beloved, we're convinced better stuff's in store for you, stuff that comes with salvation, even though we're talkin' this rough. God's not unfair, no way — He's not gonna forget your work, and the loving labor you showed for His name, servin' the saints, and still servin' 'em now. We want every single one of you to keep up that same hustle, all the way to the end, so you don't turn into a couch potato, but instead copy the folks who inherited the promises through faith and patience.

web_text: For when God made a promise to Abraham, since he could swear by no one greater, he swore by himself, saying, “Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.” Thus, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise. For men indeed swear by a greater one, and in every dispute of theirs the oath is final for confirmation.
slang_text: 'Cause when God made a promise to Abraham, since He couldn't swear by anybody greater than Himself, He swore by Himself, sayin', "I'm gonna bless you for sure, fer real, and multiply you like crazy." So after patchin' through it patiently, Abraham got the promise, no doubt about it. People swear by somebody greater than themselves, and in any argument, an oath settles it once and for all.

web_text: In this way God, being determined to show more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the immutability of his counsel, interposed with an oath; that by two immutable things, in which it is impossible for God to lie, we may have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to take hold of the hope set before us. This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast and entering into that which is within the veil; where as a forerunner Jesus entered for us, having become a high priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.
slang_text: That's how God, dead-set on showin' the promise's heirs just how unchangeable His plan is, backed it up with an oath — so by two things that can't ever change, since it's flat-out impossible for God to lie, we'd have huge, epic encouragement, us who ran for refuge to grab onto the hope right in front of us. We've got this hope like an anchor for our souls, solid and steady, reachin' right on into the space behind the veil — where Jesus already went ahead of us as our scout, becomin' a high priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek, fer sure.

web_text: For this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings and blessed him, to whom also Abraham divided a tenth part of all (being first, by interpretation, “king of righteousness”, and then also “king of Salem”, which means “king of peace”; without father, without mother, without genealogy, having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but made like the Son of God), remains a priest continually. Now consider how great this man was, to whom even Abraham, the patriarch, gave a tenth out of the best plunder.
slang_text: So this Melchizedek, king of Salem, priest of God Most High, who met Abraham comin' back from wipin' out the kings and blessed him, and Abraham split off a tenth of everything to him, no lie (translatin' out to "king of righteousness" first, and then "king of Salem," which means "king of peace" — no father, no mother, no family tree, no start date, no end date, made just like the Son of God) — this dude stays a priest forever. Now think about how epic this guy was, that even Abraham the patriarch handed over a tenth of the best loot to him.

web_text: They indeed of the sons of Levi who receive the priest’s office have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brothers, though these have come out of the body of Abraham, but he whose genealogy is not counted from them has accepted tithes from Abraham, and has blessed him who has the promises. But without any dispute the lesser is blessed by the greater. Here people who die receive tithes, but there one receives tithes of whom it is testified that he lives.
slang_text: Sure, the sons of Levi who become priests got a commandment to collect tithes from the people, matchin' the law, meanin' from their own brothers, even though they all came from Abraham's own bloodline. But this guy, whose family tree don't even trace back to 'em, still collected tithes from Abraham, and blessed the guy who's got the promises, fer sure. And no way around it — the lesser gets blessed by the greater. Here, priests who die collect the tithes; there, one guy collects 'em who's testified to be alive, straight up.

web_text: We can say that through Abraham even Levi, who receives tithes, has paid tithes, for he was yet in the body of his father when Melchizedek met him. Now if there were perfection through the Levitical priesthood (for under it the people have received the law), what further need was there for another priest to arise after the order of Melchizedek, and not be called after the order of Aaron? For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.
slang_text: You could even say that through Abraham, Levi himself — the guy who collects tithes — actually paid tithes, weird as that sounds, 'cause he was still in his ancestor's body when Melchizedek met Abraham. Now if the Levitical priesthood coulda made everything perfect (and the people got the whole law under that system), why'd we need another priest to show up in the order of Melchizedek instead of Aaron's order? 'Cause when the priesthood changes, the law's gotta change right along with it, no doubt about it.

web_text: For he of whom these things are said belongs to another tribe, from which no one has officiated at the altar. For it is evident that our Lord has sprung out of Judah, about which tribe Moses spoke nothing concerning priesthood. This is yet more abundantly evident, if after the likeness of Melchizedek there arises another priest, who has been made, not after the law of a fleshly commandment, but after the power of an endless life:
slang_text: 'Cause the guy this stuff's talkin' about belongs to a totally different tribe, one where nobody's ever served at the altar. It's plain as day our Lord came outta Judah, and Moses never said a word about priests comin' from that tribe. And that's way clearer, fer sure, once you see another priest showin' up who looks like Melchizedek — made not by some rulebook about fleshly ancestry, but by the sheer power of a life that never ends:

'cause it's testified: "You're a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek." So the old rulebook gets canceled, no lie, 'cause it was weak and bogus (the law never made nothin' perfect), and a better hope gets brought in instead, one we can use to get close to God. And it's not like He got made priest without an oath backin' it up —

web_text:
(for they indeed have been made priests without an oath), but he with an oath by him that says of him,
“The Lord swore and will not change his mind,
‘You are a priest forever,
according to the order of Melchizedek.’” By so much, Jesus has become the collateral of a better covenant. Many, indeed, have been made priests, because they are hindered from continuing by death. But he, because he lives forever, has his priesthood unchangeable.
slang_text: (the other priests got made without any oath at all) but He got made priest with an oath, from the one who told Him, "The Lord swore it and He's not gonna change His mind: 'You're a priest forever, in the order of Melchizedek.'" That's how much better, how much more righteous, a covenant Jesus backs up. Sure, a ton of guys got made priests, but death always cut their run short. But Him, since He lives forever, His priesthood never changes, no way, period.

web_text: Therefore he is also able to save to the uttermost those who draw near to God through him, seeing that he lives forever to make intercession for them. For such a high priest was fitting for us: holy, guiltless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and made higher than the heavens; who doesn’t need, like those high priests, to offer up sacrifices daily, first for his own sins, and then for the sins of the people. For he did this once for all, when he offered up himself. For the law appoints men as high priests who have weakness, but the word of the oath which came after the law appoints a Son forever who has been perfected.
slang_text: So He's able to save all the way, no limits, everybody who comes to God through Him, since He lives forever to keep pleadin' for us, fer sure. This is exactly the kind of high priest we needed: holy, blameless, spotless, set apart from sinners, lifted way above the heavens; who don't gotta offer sacrifices every single day like those other high priests, first for His own sins and then for the people's — 'cause He did it once, for good, when He offered up Himself. The law appoints weak, flawed guys as high priests, but the oath that came after the law appoints a Son who's been made perfect, forever, no doubt about it.

web_text: Now in the things which we are saying, the main point is this. We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens, a servant of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man. For every high priest is appointed to offer both gifts and sacrifices. Therefore it is necessary that this high priest also have something to offer. For if he were on earth, he would not be a priest at all, seeing there are priests who offer the gifts according to the law;
slang_text: Now, here's the main point of everything we're sayin', fer sure: we've got a high priest like this, who sat down at the right hand of the Majesty's throne up in heaven, servin' in the sanctuary, the real tabernacle the Lord Himself pitched, not some human. Every high priest gets appointed to offer gifts and sacrifices — so this high priest's gotta have somethin' to offer too, no lie. If He were down here on earth, He wouldn't even be a priest, since there's already priests offerin' gifts accordin' to the law;

who serve a copy and shadow of the heavenly stuff, same as Moses got warned by God when he was fixin' to build the tabernacle — God said, "Look, build everything exactly matchin' the pattern I showed you on the mountain, no shortcuts." But now He's landed a way better ministry, epic even, matchin' up with the fact that He's mediator of a better covenant, built on better promises. 'Cause if that first covenant had been flawless, nobody woulda gone lookin' for a second one. But God found fault with the people and said, "Check it out, the days are comin'," says the Lord, "when I'm gonna make a new covenant with the house of Israel and the house of Judah;

not like the covenant I made with their ancestors, back when I took 'em by the hand to lead 'em outta Egypt — 'cause they didn't stick with my covenant, so I let 'em go their own way," says the Lord. "This is the covenant I'm gonna make with the house of Israel after those days," says the Lord: "I'm gonna put my laws right in their minds, write 'em straight on their hearts, for real. I'll be their God, and they'll be my people. They won't gotta teach their neighbor or their own brother, sayin', 'Know the Lord,' 'cause every last one of 'em's gonna know me already, from the least to the greatest. I'm gonna show mercy on their wrongdoin'. I'm not gonna remember their sins and lawless deeds no more, no lie."

web_text: In that he says, “A new covenant”, he has made the first old. But that which is becoming old and grows aged is near to vanishing away.
slang_text: By callin' it "a new covenant," He made the first one old news, done and dusted. And whatever's gettin' old and worn out is right on the edge of fadin' away completely, fer sure.

web_text: Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service, and an earthly sanctuary. For a tabernacle was prepared. In the first part were the lamp stand, the table, and the show bread; which is called the Holy Place. After the second veil was the tabernacle which is called the Holy of Holies, having a golden altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant overlaid on all sides with gold, in which was a golden pot holding the manna, Aaron’s rod that budded, and the tablets of the covenant;
slang_text: Now the first covenant had its own rules for worship, and an earthly sanctuary too, righteous as it was. A tabernacle got set up. In the first section sat the lampstand, the table, and the showbread — that's called the Holy Place. Behind the second veil sat the section called the Holy of Holies, with a gold altar of incense, and the ark of the covenant, plated in gold on every side, holdin' a gold jar of manna, Aaron's rod that sprouted, and the tablets of the covenant;

web_text: and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy seat, of which things we can’t speak now in detail. Now these things having been thus prepared, the priests go in continually into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the services, but into the second the high priest alone, once in the year, not without blood, which he offers for himself, and for the errors of the people. The Holy Spirit is indicating this, that the way into the Holy Place wasn’t yet revealed while the first tabernacle was still standing;
slang_text: and above it, glorious cherubim overshadowin' the mercy seat — we can't get into all the details on that right now, no lie. With all this set up like that, the priests keep goin' into the first section nonstop, doin' their work, but only the high priest goes into the second section, once a year, and never without blood, which he offers for himself and for the people's screwups. The Holy Spirit's tryin' to tell us somethin' with this: the way into the Holy Place wasn't revealed yet while the first tabernacle was still standin', fer sure;

web_text: which is a symbol of the present age, where gifts and sacrifices are offered that are incapable, concerning the conscience, of making the worshiper perfect; being only (with meats and drinks and various washings) fleshly ordinances, imposed until a time of reformation. But Christ having come as a high priest of the coming good things, through the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation, nor yet through the blood of goats and calves, but through his own blood, entered in once for all into the Holy Place, having obtained eternal redemption.
slang_text: which is a symbol pointin' to right now — where gifts and sacrifices get offered that can't ever make the worshiper's conscience perfect. It's all just physical rules (about food, drink, and washin' rituals), pretty bogus if you think about it, meant to hold things over till reform time got here. But Christ showed up as high priest of the good stuff that's comin', through a bigger, more perfect, totally tubular tabernacle nobody built with human hands, not even part of this creation — and He didn't go in through the blood of goats and calves, but through His own blood, walkin' into the Holy Place once, for good, scorin' eternal redemption.

web_text: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and the ashes of a heifer sprinkling those who have been defiled, sanctify to the cleanness of the flesh: how much more will the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without defect to God, cleanse your conscience from dead works to serve the living God? For this reason he is the mediator of a new covenant, since a death has occurred for the redemption of the transgressions that were under the first covenant, that those who have been called may receive the promise of the eternal inheritance. For where a last will and testament is, there must of necessity be the death of him who made it.
slang_text: 'Cause if the blood of goats and bulls, plus the ashes of a heifer sprinkled on defiled folks, can clean 'em up on the outside — how much more's the blood of Christ gonna do, epic as it is, who offered Himself spotless to God through the eternal Spirit, cleanin' your conscience from dead works so you can serve the livin' God? That's exactly why He's the mediator of a new covenant, since a death already happened to buy back the sins committed under the first covenant, so the ones who got called could score the promised eternal inheritance. 'Cause wherever there's a will and testament, the guy who wrote it's gotta die first, no doubt about it.

web_text: For a will is in force where there has been death, for it is never in force while he who made it lives. Therefore even the first covenant has not been dedicated without blood. For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of the calves and the goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book itself and all the people, saying, “This is the blood of the covenant which God has commanded you.”
slang_text: A will only kicks in once somebody's died — it never kicks in while the guy who wrote it's still alive, fer sure. So even the first covenant never got dedicated without blood. When Moses laid out every commandment from the law to all the people, he grabbed the blood of calves and goats, mixed with water, scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled it on the scroll itself and all the people, sayin', "This is the blood of the covenant God's ordered you to keep, no lie."

web_text: Moreover he sprinkled the tabernacle and all the vessels of the ministry in the same way with the blood. According to the law, nearly everything is cleansed with blood, and apart from shedding of blood there is no remission. It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these. For Christ hasn’t entered into holy places made with hands, which are representations of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us;
slang_text: He sprinkled the tabernacle and every tool used for ministry with that same blood too. Under the law, just about everything gets cleaned with blood, and without blood gettin' spilled, there's no forgiveness at all, no way around it. So it had to be that the earthly copies of the heavenly stuff got cleaned this way, but the heavenly stuff itself needed way better, way righteous sacrifices than these. 'Cause Christ never walked into some human-built holy place, just a bogus copy of the real thing — He walked right into heaven itself, showin' up in front of God for our sake;

web_text: nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy place year by year with blood not his own, or else he must have suffered often since the foundation of the world. But now once at the end of the ages, he has been revealed to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself. Inasmuch as it is appointed for men to die once, and after this, judgment, so Christ also, having been offered once to bear the sins of many, will appear a second time, without sin, to those who are eagerly waiting for him for salvation.
slang_text: and He didn't gotta keep offerin' Himself over and over, like the high priest goin' into the holy place year after year with blood that ain't his own — or He'd have had to suffer over and over since the foundation of the world, way harsh. But instead, once, right at the end of the ages, He got revealed to wipe out sin by sacrificin' Himself. Same as it's set for people to die once, and then face judgment, Christ too got offered once to carry the sins of a ton of folks, and He's gonna show up a second time, sin-free, fer sure, for everybody who's eagerly waitin' on Him for salvation.

web_text: For the law, having a shadow of the good to come, not the very image of the things, can never with the same sacrifices year by year, which they offer continually, make perfect those who draw near. Or else wouldn’t they have ceased to be offered, because the worshipers, having been once cleansed, would have had no more consciousness of sins? But in those sacrifices there is a yearly reminder of sins. For it is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats should take away sins.
slang_text: 'Cause the law's just a shadow of the good stuff comin', not the real deal itself, and it can never make the folks who draw near perfect with the same sacrifices, offered year after year, over and over, no matter how you slice it. Or else wouldn't they have stopped offerin' 'em, since the worshipers, once cleaned, wouldn't feel guilty about sins no more? But instead, those sacrifices just remind everybody of sins every single year. 'Cause it's flat-out impossible, no way, for the blood of bulls and goats to actually take away sins.

web_text:
Therefore when he comes into the world, he says,
“Sacrifice and offering you didn’t desire,
but you prepared a body for me. You had no pleasure in whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin. Then I said, ‘Behold, I have come (in the scroll of the book it is written of me)
to do your will, O God.’” Previously saying, “Sacrifices and offerings and whole burnt offerings and sacrifices for sin you didn’t desire, neither had pleasure in them” (those which are offered according to the law),
slang_text: So when He comes into the world, He says, "Sacrifice and offerin' you never wanted, no lie, but you got a body ready for me instead. Burnt offerings and sin sacrifices never made you happy. So I said, 'Look, here I am (it's written about me in the scroll) — I've come to do your will, O God.'" First He says, "Sacrifices, offerings, burnt offerings, and sin sacrifices, you never wanted 'em, never got happy over 'em, for real" (the ones offered accordin' to the law),

web_text: then he has said, “Behold, I have come to do your will.” He takes away the first, that he may establish the second, by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all. Every priest indeed stands day by day serving and often offering the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins, but he, when he had offered one sacrifice for sins forever, sat down on the right hand of God;
slang_text: and then He says, "Look, I've come to do your will." He wipes out the first system, so He can set up the second — and that will is exactly how we got made holy, epic and complete, through the offerin' of Jesus Christ's body, once for all. Every priest stands there day after day, servin', offerin' up the same sacrifices over and over, which can never take away sins — but Him, He offered one sacrifice for sins, forever, and sat down at God's right hand, straight up;

web_text:
from that time waiting until his enemies are made the footstool of his feet. For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified. The Holy Spirit also testifies to us, for after saying, “This is the covenant that I will make with them:
‘After those days,’ says the Lord,
‘I will put my laws on their heart,
I will also write them on their mind;’”
then he says,
slang_text: waitin' since then till His enemies get made His own footstool, no doubt. 'Cause with one offerin', He's perfected forever the folks who're gettin' made holy. The Holy Spirit backs this up too, sayin' first, "This is the covenant I'm gonna make with 'em after those days," says the Lord: "I'll put my laws right on their hearts, I'll write 'em on their minds too" — and then He says,

web_text: “I will remember their sins and their iniquities no more.” Now where remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin. Having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus, by the way which he dedicated for us, a new and living way, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;
slang_text: "I'm not gonna remember their sins and their wrongdoin' no more." And where those get forgiven, there's no need for a sin offerin' no more, no joke. So, brothers, since we got boldness to walk right into the holy place through Jesus' blood, through a totally new, livin' road He carved out for us, straight through the veil — that's His own flesh, we're talkin' about;

web_text: and having a great priest over God’s house, let’s draw near with a true heart in fullness of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and having our body washed with pure water, let us hold fast the confession of our hope without wavering; for he who promised is faithful. Let us consider how to provoke one another to love and good works,
slang_text: and since we've got a righteous priest runnin' God's whole house, let's draw near with genuine hearts, faith fully loaded, our hearts sprinkled clean from a guilty conscience and our bodies washed with pure water. Let's hold onto our confession of hope, tight, no wobblin', 'cause the One who promised is faithful, straight up. Let's think about how to spur each other on toward love and good works,

web_text: not forsaking our own assembling together, as the custom of some is, but exhorting one another; and so much the more, as you see the Day approaching. For if we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins, but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and a fierceness of fire which will devour the adversaries. A man who disregards Moses’ law dies without compassion on the word of two or three witnesses.
slang_text: not bailin' on gettin' together like some folks make a habit of, but encouragin' each other, and even more, since you can see the Day gettin' closer, no doubt. 'Cause if we go on sinnin' on purpose after we've already gotten the truth, there's no sacrifice left for sins — just a terrifyin' expectation of judgment and a ragin' fire that's gonna devour God's enemies, way harsh. Anybody who blows off Moses' law dies without mercy, on the word of two or three witnesses.

web_text: How much worse punishment do you think he will be judged worthy of who has trodden under foot the Son of God, and has counted the blood of the covenant with which he was sanctified an unholy thing, and has insulted the Spirit of grace? For we know him who said, “Vengeance belongs to me,” says the Lord, “I will repay.” Again, “The Lord will judge his people.” It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God. But remember the former days, in which, after you were enlightened, you endured a great struggle with sufferings;
slang_text: So how much worse a punishment do you figure somebody deserves who's stomped all over the Son of God, treated the covenant blood that made him holy like it's grody trash, and totally dissed the Spirit of grace? 'Cause we know the One who said, "Vengeance is mine," says the Lord, "I'll pay it back." And again, "The Lord's gonna judge His own people." It's a terrifyin' thing to fall into the hands of the livin' God, no foolin'. But remember them earlier days, when after you first got enlightened, you put up with a huge struggle full of sufferin';

web_text: partly, being exposed to both reproaches and oppressions; and partly, becoming partakers with those who were treated so. For you both had compassion on me in my chains, and joyfully accepted the plundering of your possessions, knowing that you have for yourselves a better possession and an enduring one in the heavens. Therefore don’t throw away your boldness, which has a great reward. For you need endurance so that, having done the will of God, you may receive the promise.
slang_text: partly exposed yourselves to insults and rough treatment, and partly stood with folks who were gettin' treated that way too. You had compassion on me in my chains, and you were even stoked when your stuff got plundered, knowin' you got somethin' way better waitin' for you, somethin' that lasts, up in the heavens. So don't go tossin' out your boldness — it's got a huge, epic reward attached. You need to stick it out, so once you've done what God wants, you'll get the promise, for real.

web_text:
“In a very little while,
he who comes will come, and will not wait. But the righteous will live by faith.
If he shrinks back, my soul has no pleasure in him.” But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith to the saving of the soul.
slang_text: "In just a little while, the one who's comin' is gonna come, and He's not gonna wait around, no way. But the righteous are gonna live by faith. And if anybody shrinks back, my soul's not gonna be happy with 'em." But we're not the shrink-back-into-destruction crowd — we're the faith crowd, savin' our souls, no doubt about it.

web_text: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen. For by this, the elders obtained testimony. By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God, so that what is seen has not been made out of things which are visible. By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, through which he had testimony given to him that he was righteous, God testifying with respect to his gifts; and through it he, being dead, still speaks.
slang_text: Now faith's the guarantee of stuff we're hopin' for, proof of stuff we can't even see, no doubt. That's how the elders of old got their testimony. By faith, we get it that the universe got framed up by God's own word, so what we see didn't come outta stuff we could already see. By faith, Abel offered God a way more excellent sacrifice than Cain, and that's how he got testified as righteous, with God vouchin' for his gifts — and through that, even though he's dead, he's still speakin' to us today, straight up.

web_text: By faith, Enoch was taken away, so that he wouldn’t see death, and he was not found, because God translated him. For he has had testimony given to him that before his translation he had been well pleasing to God. Without faith it is impossible to be well pleasing to him, for he who comes to God must believe that he exists, and that he is a rewarder of those who seek him. By faith, Noah, being warned about things not yet seen, moved with godly fear, prepared a ship for the saving of his house, through which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is according to faith. By faith, Abraham, when he was called, obeyed to go out to the place which he was to receive for an inheritance. He went out, not knowing where he went.
slang_text: By faith, Enoch got taken away so he never had to see death, and nobody could find him, 'cause God whisked him off, just like that. He got testified as pleasin' to God before he even got taken. Without faith, it's flat-out impossible to please Him, 'cause anybody comin' to God's gotta believe He's real, and that He rewards everybody who's genuinely lookin' for Him. By faith, Noah, warned about stuff nobody could see yet, got movin' outta godly fear and built a ship to save his household, and that's how he condemned the world and inherited the righteousness that comes through faith. By faith, Abraham, when he got called, obeyed and headed out to the place he was gonna inherit — headed out not even knowin' where he was goin', trippin' the whole way, probably.

web_text: By faith, he lived as an alien in the land of promise, as in a land not his own, dwelling in tents, with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise. For he looked for the city which has the foundations, whose builder and maker is God. By faith, even Sarah herself received power to conceive, and she bore a child when she was past age, since she counted him faithful who had promised. Therefore as many as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as innumerable as the sand which is by the sea shore, were fathered by one man, and him as good as dead.
slang_text: By faith, he lived like an outsider in the Promised Land, like it wasn't even his own, campin' out in tents, right alongside Isaac and Jacob, who inherited that same promise with him. 'Cause he was lookin' ahead to the city with the real foundations, the one God Himself designed and built, for real. By faith, even Sarah got the power to conceive, havin' a kid way past her prime, 'cause she figured the One who promised was faithful. So from one guy, basically dead already as far as havin' kids went, came descendants as countless as the stars in the sky, as countless as the sand along the seashore, no joke.

web_text: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them and embraced them from afar, and having confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth. For those who say such things make it clear that they are seeking a country of their own. If indeed they had been thinking of that country from which they went out, they would have had enough time to return. But now they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one. Therefore God is not ashamed of them, to be called their God, for he has prepared a city for them.
slang_text: Every last one of these folks died still holdin' onto faith, never gettin' the promises in their own hands, but seein' 'em and embracin' 'em from way off in the distance, admittin' they were just strangers and travelers passin' through this earth. Folks who say stuff like that are makin' it obvious they're lookin' for a home of their own, no doubt. If they'd been thinkin' about the place they left behind, they woulda had plenty of time to head back. But instead, they wanted somethin' better — a totally righteous heavenly home. That's why God's not ashamed to be called their God — He's already got a city ready for 'em, no foolin'.

web_text: By faith, Abraham, being tested, offered up Isaac. Yes, he who had gladly received the promises was offering up his one and only son, to whom it was said, “your offspring will be accounted as from Isaac”; concluding that God is able to raise up even from the dead. Figuratively speaking, he also did receive him back from the dead. By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even concerning things to come.
slang_text: By faith, Abraham, gettin' put to the test — talk about way harsh — offered up Isaac. Yeah, the guy who'd gladly received the promises was offerin' up his one and only son — the same kid God had already told him, "your descendants are gonna come through Isaac" — figurin' God could raise the dead back up, no problem. And in a way, that's exactly what happened, gettin' Isaac back like from the dead, for real. By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau, even talkin' about stuff still comin' down the road.

web_text: By faith, Jacob, when he was dying, blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshiped, leaning on the top of his staff. By faith, Joseph, when his end was near, made mention of the departure of the children of Israel; and gave instructions concerning his bones. By faith, Moses, when he was born, was hidden for three months by his parents, because they saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment. By faith, Moses, when he had grown up, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter,
slang_text: By faith, Jacob, on his deathbed, blessed both of Joseph's sons, worshippin', leanin' on the top of his staff. By faith, Joseph, when his own end got close, talked about Israel's kids finally bouncin' outta Egypt, and gave orders about what to do with his own bones. By faith, Moses, when he got born, got hidden by his parents for three months, 'cause they could tell he was a beautiful kid, and they weren't scared of the king's order, no way. By faith, Moses, once he grew up, refused to be called Pharaoh's daughter's son,

web_text: choosing rather to share ill treatment with God’s people, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a time; accounting the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he looked to the reward. By faith, he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible. By faith, he kept the Passover, and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn should not touch them.
slang_text: choosin' instead to put up with rough treatment alongside God's people, rather than enjoy sin's cheap, bogus thrills for a little while. He counted Christ's reproach as way richer than all Egypt's treasures, 'cause he had his eyes on the reward. By faith, he left Egypt, not scared one bit of the king's fury, hangin' tough like he could see the invisible God right in front of him. By faith, he kept the Passover and sprinkled the blood, so the destroyer of the firstborn wouldn't touch 'em, no doubt about it.

web_text: By faith, they passed through the Red Sea as on dry land. When the Egyptians tried to do so, they were swallowed up. By faith, the walls of Jericho fell down, after they had been encircled for seven days. By faith, Rahab the prostitute, didn’t perish with those who were disobedient, having received the spies in peace. What more shall I say? For the time would fail me if I told of Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets;
slang_text: By faith, they walked right through the Red Sea like it was dry land, straight up. When the Egyptians tried the same thing, they got swallowed whole. By faith, the walls of Jericho came tumblin' down, after gettin' circled for seven days straight. By faith, Rahab the prostitute didn't perish with the disobedient crowd, 'cause she welcomed the spies in peace. What more do I even need to say? I'd run outta time if I started tellin' you about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets, no lie;

web_text: who, through faith subdued kingdoms, worked out righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions, quenched the power of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, from weakness were made strong, grew mighty in war, and caused foreign armies to flee. Women received their dead by resurrection. Others were tortured, not accepting their deliverance, that they might obtain a better resurrection. Others were tried by mocking and scourging, yes, moreover by bonds and imprisonment.
slang_text: who, through faith, took down entire kingdoms, pulled off righteousness, scored promises, shut the mouths of lions, snuffed out ragin' fires, dodged the edge of the sword, went from weak to strong, turned mighty in battle, and sent whole foreign armies runnin', epic style. Women got their dead back through resurrection. Others got tortured, refusin' to take the easy way out, holdin' out for an even better resurrection. Others got tested through mockery and whippings, and even chains and prison time, way harsh as that was.

web_text: They were stoned. They were sawn apart. They were tempted. They were slain with the sword. They went around in sheep skins and in goat skins; being destitute, afflicted, ill-treated (of whom the world was not worthy), wandering in deserts, mountains, caves, and the holes of the earth. These all, having had testimony given to them through their faith, didn’t receive the promise, God having provided some better thing concerning us, so that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
slang_text: They got stoned. They got sawed in half. They got tempted. They got killed with the sword. Way harsh, all of it. They wandered around wrapped in sheepskins and goatskins, dirt poor, sufferin', treated like garbage — and this world wasn't even worthy of 'em — driftin' through deserts, mountains, caves, and holes in the ground. Every last one of 'em got testified as faithful, but none of 'em got the promise in their own hands, 'cause God had somethin' better lined up involvin' us, so that without us, they wouldn't get made complete, for real.

web_text: Therefore let us also, seeing we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, lay aside every weight and the sin which so easily entangles us, and let us run with perseverance the race that is set before us, looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising its shame, and has sat down at the right hand of the throne of God. For consider him who has endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, that you don’t grow weary, fainting in your souls. You have not yet resisted to blood, striving against sin;
slang_text: So since we're surrounded by this huge, epic crowd of witnesses cheerin' us on, let's ditch every extra weight and the sin that trips us up so easy, and let's run this race set out in front of us with everything we got, keepin' our eyes on Jesus, the one who started and finished our faith — who put up with the cross for the joy waitin' on the other side, not even carin' about the shame of it, and sat down at the right hand of God's throne. Think about Him, who put up with sinners comin' at Him hard, way harsh, so you don't burn out and give up in your own souls. You haven't even bled yet, fightin' against sin;

web_text:
and you have forgotten the exhortation which reasons with you as with children,
“My son, don’t take lightly the chastening of the Lord,
nor faint when you are reproved by him; For whom the Lord loves, he chastens,
and scourges every son whom he receives.” It is for discipline that you endure. God deals with you as with children, for what son is there whom his father doesn’t discipline? But if you are without discipline, of which all have been made partakers, then are you illegitimate, and not children.
slang_text: and you've forgotten the encouragement that talks to you like you're kids: "My son, don't blow off the Lord's discipline, and don't give up when He calls you out — 'cause the Lord disciplines whoever He loves, and whips every son He takes in." No lie. You're puttin' up with this for the sake of discipline. God's treatin' you like His own kids — what kid's dad doesn't discipline him? But if you never get disciplined, somethin' everybody else gets, then you're not really His kids, you're just some outsider, fer sure.

web_text: Furthermore, we had the fathers of our flesh to chasten us, and we paid them respect. Shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live? For they indeed, for a few days, punished us as seemed good to them; but he for our profit, that we may be partakers of his holiness. All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been exercised thereby. Therefore lift up the hands that hang down and the feeble knees,
slang_text: On top of that, we had our own earthly dads disciplinin' us, and we respected 'em for it. So shouldn't we way more so yield to the Father of spirits, and actually live? Our earthly dads disciplined us for a few years, whatever seemed right to them at the time — but God does it for our own good, so we can share in His holiness. No discipline feels fun while it's happenin' — it feels way harsh — but later on it grows the peaceful fruit of righteousness in whoever went through the trainin'. So lift up those droopy hands and those wobbly knees, no more mopin' around,

web_text: and make straight paths for your feet, so what is lame may not be dislocated, but rather be healed. Follow after peace with all men, and the sanctification without which no man will see the Lord, looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and many be defiled by it; lest there be any sexually immoral person, or profane person, like Esau, who sold his birthright for one meal.
slang_text: and cut yourselves a straight path so whatever's limpin' doesn't get worse, but gets healed instead. Chase after peace with everybody, and chase holiness too — without it, no way you're ever gonna see the Lord. Watch out real careful that nobody comes up short of God's grace; that no bitter root sprouts up and causes trouble, defilin' a ton of folks; that nobody turns sexually immoral or godless, like Esau, who sold his birthright for one lousy, bogus meal.

web_text: For you know that even when he afterward desired to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, for he found no place for a change of mind though he sought it diligently with tears. For you have not come to a mountain that might be touched, and that burned with fire, and to blackness, darkness, storm, the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which those who heard it begged that not one more word should be spoken to them, for they could not stand that which was commanded, “If even an animal touches the mountain, it shall be stoned”;
slang_text: You already know that later on, when he wanted the blessin' back, he got shot down, way harsh — he couldn't find a way to change things, even though he went huntin' for it hard, cryin' his eyes out. 'Cause you guys haven't come to some mountain you could actually touch, burnin' with fire, wrapped in blackness, darkness, a storm, a trumpet blastin', and a voice speakin' words — and everybody who heard it begged for not one more word to get spoken, 'cause they couldn't handle the order: "Even if an animal touches this mountain, it's gettin' stoned, no exceptions";

web_text: and so fearful was the appearance that Moses said, “I am terrified and trembling.” But you have come to Mount Zion, and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to innumerable multitudes of angels, to the festal gathering and assembly of the firstborn who are enrolled in heaven, to God the Judge of all, to the spirits of just men made perfect, to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling that speaks better than that of Abel.
slang_text: and the whole sight was so terrifyin' that even Moses said, "I'm scared outta my mind and shakin'." But you guys have come to Mount Zion instead, to the city of the livin' God, the heavenly Jerusalem, to countless angels partyin' on, to the gathered assembly of the firstborn already registered in heaven, to God the Judge of everybody, to the spirits of righteous folks made perfect, to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant, and to sprinkled blood that speaks somethin' way, way better than Abel's ever did.

web_text: See that you don’t refuse him who speaks. For if they didn’t escape when they refused him who warned on the earth, how much more will we not escape who turn away from him who warns from heaven, whose voice shook the earth then, but now he has promised, saying, “Yet once more I will shake not only the earth, but also the heavens.” This phrase, “Yet once more”, signifies the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that have been made, that those things which are not shaken may remain. Therefore, receiving a Kingdom that can’t be shaken, let us have grace, through which we serve God acceptably, with reverence and awe,
slang_text: Make sure you don't blow off the One who's speakin', no way. 'Cause if folks couldn't escape blowin' off the warnin' God gave 'em down here on earth, how much less are we gonna escape if we turn away from the One warnin' us from heaven? His voice shook the earth back then, but now He's promised, "Just once more, I'm gonna shake not only the earth, but the heavens too." That phrase, "just once more," means He's gonna remove everything that CAN get shaken, everything that got made, so what CAN'T get shaken sticks around for good, fer sure. So since we're gettin' a Kingdom that can't ever get shaken, let's hold onto grace, and through it, serve God the right way, with reverence and awe,

web_text: for our God is a consuming fire.
slang_text: 'cause our God's a consumin' fire, no joke.

web_text: Let brotherly love continue. Don’t forget to show hospitality to strangers, for in doing so, some have entertained angels without knowing it. Remember those who are in bonds, as bound with them; and those who are ill-treated, since you are also in the body. Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled: but God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers.
slang_text: Keep that brotherly love goin' strong. Don't forget to be hospitable to strangers — some folks ended up hostin' angels without even knowin' it, no joke. Remember the ones stuck in chains, like you're chained up right along with 'em; and the ones gettin' mistreated, since you're in a body too, and it could happen to you. Let marriage stay honored across the board, and keep the marriage bed pure — 'cause God's gonna judge the sexually immoral and the cheaters, straight up.

web_text:
Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.” So that with good courage we say,
“The Lord is my helper. I will not fear.
What can man do to me?” Remember your leaders, men who spoke to you the word of God, and considering the results of their conduct, imitate their faith. Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
slang_text: Keep yourself free from lovin' money, stay content with whatever you already got, 'cause He already said, "I'm never gonna leave you, never gonna abandon you, no way." So we say, brave and confident: "The Lord's my helper. I'm not gonna be scared. What can people even do to me?" Remember your leaders, the guys who spoke God's word to you, and lookin' at how their lives turned out, copy their faith, for real. Jesus Christ is exactly the same yesterday, today, and forever, no doubt about it.

web_text: Don’t be carried away by various and strange teachings, for it is good that the heart be established by grace, not by food, through which those who were so occupied were not benefited. We have an altar from which those who serve the holy tabernacle have no right to eat. For the bodies of those animals, whose blood is brought into the holy place by the high priest as an offering for sin, are burned outside of the camp. Therefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside of the gate.
slang_text: Don't get swept off by all kinds of weird, bogus teachin's — it's good for the heart to get locked in solid by grace, not by food rules, which never did nobody chasin' 'em any good. We got an altar that the folks servin' in the tabernacle don't even have the right to eat from. 'Cause the bodies of the animals whose blood the high priest carries into the holy place as a sin offerin' get burned outside the camp. That's exactly why Jesus, so He could make the people holy through His own blood, suffered outside the city gate too, way harsh as that was.

web_text:
Let us therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach. For we don’t have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come. Through him, then, let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God
 continually, that is, the fruit of lips which proclaim allegiance to his name. But don’t forget to be doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.
slang_text: So let's go out to Him outside the camp too, carryin' His reproach with us. 'Cause we don't got a city here that lasts forever — we're lookin' ahead to the one still comin', no lie. So through Him, let's keep offerin' up a sacrifice of praise to God nonstop, the fruit of lips that proclaim loyalty to His name. But don't forget to keep doin' good and sharin' what you got, 'cause God's totally stoked on sacrifices like that.

web_text: Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch on behalf of your souls, as those who will give account, that they may do this with joy, and not with groaning, for that would be unprofitable for you. Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, desiring to live honorably in all things. I strongly urge you to do this, that I may be restored to you sooner. Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus,
slang_text: Obey your leaders and yield to 'em, 'cause they're watchin' out for your souls, knowin' they gotta answer for it — so make sure they do this with joy, not groanin' about it, 'cause that wouldn't do you no good anyway. Pray for us, 'cause we're convinced we got a clean conscience, for real, wantin' to live honorably in everything. I'm pushin' you hard to do this, so I can get back to you sooner. Now may the God of peace, who brought the great shepherd of the sheep back from the dead through the blood of an eternal covenant — our Lord Jesus —

web_text: make you complete in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen. But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation; for I have written to you in few words. Know that our brother Timothy has been freed, with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you. Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.
slang_text: make you complete in every good work for doin' His will, workin' in you whatever's pleasin' in His sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever, no doubt. Amen. I'm pushin' you, brothers, to put up with this word of encouragement, 'cause I kept it short. Know that our brother Timothy's been let go — if he shows up soon, I'll come see you with him. Greet all your leaders and all the saints. The Italians send their greetin's too, for real.

web_text: Grace be with you all. Amen.
slang_text: Grace be with all of you, no doubt. Amen.
